title: Ancylostemon aureus
taxonomy: Ancylostemon aureus is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant belongs to the genus Ancylostemon, which is part of the Melastomataceae family. Ancylostemon aureus is a relatively rare species, and its classification is more closely aligned with the broader Melastomataceae family. The specific classification of Ancylostemon aureus is not as widely documented as other species in the Melastomataceae family.

intro: Ancylostemon aureus, also known as Golden Ailanthus, is a plant species in the family Simaroubaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of fever, pain, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Golden Ailanthus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
